selector_conventions.country_select_pattern: select[name='{field}'] with ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 option values; paired input#{field}_ui typeahead is autocomplete-only (set the <select> directly, don't type in _ui)
selector_conventions.submit_button: button[name='submit'] OR input[type='submit']#submit (both present on most pages, button[name=submit value=Next] is the primary)
selector_conventions.csrf: every POST form has hidden input[name='csrfToken'] — must be preserved through Playwright navigation (don't rebuild form, fill in place)
selector_conventions.session_resume: many pages include hidden input[name='hasResumed'] boolean flag

page_detection_strategy: URL alone is insufficient — after auth, most pages live at path '/next' with the same title prefix. Detect pages by (a) form.action suffix e.g. '/save/{state}.{field}', (b) h1 text, (c) document.title e.g. 'Start - Register an email'
state_model_discovery: Form action URLs use '/save/{phase}.{field}' convention — e.g. '/save/start.contactEmail'. This is the Play Framework / UKVI 'Lifecycle' state machine. Each form submission advances to the next state determined server-side.
form_submit_quirk: document.querySelector('form').submit() fails because a button with name='submit' shadows the native submit method. Must click the submit button instead: form.querySelector('button[name="submit"], input[type="submit"]').click()
anti_bot_observed: No visible CAPTCHA on pre-auth pages. Standard cookie banner. Likely CAPTCHA or challenge after account creation — needs verification with a real account.

pages_walked.h1: Select a country to provide your biometrics
pages_walked.url_param_after_submit: country code becomes part of next-page path: /vac-information-page/{ISO3}

pages_walked.h1: Check available visa application centre locations
Apply for a UK visit visa
pages_walked.note: pass-through — just a 'Start now' submit. The form action reveals the application path id ('visit-visa-ooc-standard') which is the discriminator for this visa product.

fields.name: hasPassword
fields.purpose: discriminator for returning user — if set, this page acts as sign-in instead of register

pages_NOT_walked_pending_account: All post-registration form steps: personal details, passport, travel plans, accommodation, family, work/income, travel history, declarations, document upload, review, payment, biometric appointment booking.
These pages are gated behind email verification + account creation.
Based on the gov.uk documentation, the 222-field UK Standard Visitor seed covers these. A throwaway account + completed walk is required to capture the ~50+ form-step selectors.

required_next_step.option_a: User authorizes creating a throwaway UKVI account with a test email; Claude walks the full form and captures all selectors to extend this JSON.
required_next_step.option_b: A UK test/sandbox portal exists — user provides URL/credentials.
required_next_step.option_c: Skip recon; write form-recon script now so team can run it against a real in-progress application in a dev browser, dumping selectors to JSON.
required_next_step.recommendation: Option C + Option A — ship the recon script first so it's reusable for future maintenance (selectors drift), then run it end-to-end once with a throwaway account.

notes_for_selector_builder.name_selector_fallback: input[name='{fieldName}'] — reliable since name attributes are stable across UKVI releases
notes_for_selector_builder.country_fill_approach: await page.locator('select[name="countryCode"]').selectOption('USA') — do NOT type in the _ui field
notes_for_selector_builder.csrf_preservation: Never clear/rebuild the form; fill in place so csrfToken + externalReference hidden fields remain intact
notes_for_selector_builder.post_redirect_detection: After submit, wait for h1 change OR for form.action to change. Don't rely on URL change — URL often stays at /next.
